title:
Lesson 1:
I Love It / I Like It
description: 'Bayılıyorum' = I love it / I'm crazy about it (stronger). 'Seviyorum' = I love/like it. 'Çok seviyorum' = I like it a lot. 'Hoşuma gidiyor' = I like it (it pleases me).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you try the new restaurant?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I love it! The food is amazing.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you like the dessert too?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Çok sevdim. Bu hafta tekrar gideceğim.
dialogue.english: I liked it a lot. I'm going back this week.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Ben de gelmek istiyorum! Yeni yerler denemek hoşuma gidiyor.
dialogue.english: I want to come too! I like trying new places.

vocabulary.english: I love it / I'm crazy about it
I like it / I love it
I like it / it pleases me

title:
Lesson 2:
It Bothers Me / My Mood
description: 'Rahatsız ediyor beni' = It bothers me. 'Çok rahatsız ediyor' = It really bothers me. 'Keyfim yerinde' = I'm in a good mood. 'Keyifim yok' or 'Moralim bozuk' = I'm in a bad mood.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you okay? You look bothered.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Moralim bozuk. Gürültü çok rahatsız ediyor beni.
dialogue.english: I'm in a bad mood. The noise really bothers me.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I understand. Can I do anything?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Hayır, olsun. Yarın keyfim yerinde olur.
dialogue.english: No, it's okay. Tomorrow I'll be in a good mood.
